latest.title: Coral reefs are cities built by tiny animals
latest.script_key: coral reefs reveal the reef city first watch the reef city first because coral animals build hard skeletons that stack into habitat fish algae and countless small creatures then use that structure like a city the color sits on top of engineering which habitat next

latest.title: Lightning makes air explode into thunder
latest.script_key: lightning turns air into a shock wave watch the flash first because lightning heats a narrow path of air extremely fast that air expands suddenly and the pressure wave reaches us as thunder the sound is the atmosphere snapping back which storm signal next

latest.title: Penguins trap air bubbles under feathers
latest.script_key: penguins carry tiny air pockets into the water watch the feathers before the dive because penguins trap air that can help with insulation and speed when bubbles release the water drag changes around the body the smooth swim starts in the feather coat which polar trick next

latest.title: Trees record wet years inside their rings
latest.script_key: trees keep weather history in their rings watch the trunk pattern because each growing season leaves a new layer wide rings often mean easier growth while thin rings can mark stress that is why an old tree can become a climate diary which hidden record should come next

latest.title: Fossils turn old bones into time clues
latest.script_key: fossils can preserve a clue from deep time watch the shape in the rock because fossils form when buried remains leave mineral records behind the original body is gone but the pattern can reveal age habitat and behavior which ancient clue should we read next

latest.title: Plants use trap hairs to count touches before snapping shut
latest.script_key: plants use trap hairs to count touches before closing watch the trap hairs because the plant waits for more than one touch before spending energy on a snap that delay helps it ignore rain and debris then close when prey is real which plant trick should we decode next

latest.title: Dolphins swim in a straight line for a reason
latest.script_key: dolphins swim in a straight line because the leader s sonar works best when everyone stays close their clicks bounce between bodies painting a 3d map of the ocean floor that s why they don t spread out like fish ever wonder if you d be the one slowing them down

latest.title: Brown bears hunt like silent assassins
latest.script_key: they re not roaring hunters watch this bear s face no snarl just focus that s because it s counting on stealth not teeth its black nose is the only part that moves when it s stalking because prey doesn t hear a sound until it s too late

latest.title: The moon keeps one face turned toward Earth
latest.script_key: the moon shows us one face for a reason watch the moon s position because its rotation and orbit are locked together it spins once in about the same time it circles earth so the same side keeps facing us that is synchronized motion not stillness which orbit clue next

latest.title: Ladybug vs ladybird the naming trap
latest.script_key: this one s not what you think ladybugs are ladybirds but the name split because of a war napoleon hated english names so britain called them ladybirds after mary not bugs that s why americans kept the word bug and everyone else went with bird would you call them either

latest.title: Whales teach aquarium divers underwater
latest.script_key: a whale rolls over and gently taps a diver s mask that s why the divers back up because the whale s pectoral fin those huge wings are longer than a bus i love this one would you float still if a whale came that close

latest.title: Storm clouds reveal a storm's heat engine
latest.script_key: storm clouds show where a storm is feeding watch the spiral shape because warm ocean air rises near the center and releases heat as clouds build rotation organizes that energy into bands from above the storm is not random it is an engine which satellite clue next

latest.title: Penguins trap air bubbles under feathers
latest.script_key: penguins carry tiny air pockets into the water watch the feathers before the dive because penguins trap air that can help with insulation and speed when bubbles release the water drag changes around the body the smooth swim starts in the feather coat which polar trick next

latest.title: Horses point their ears toward what matters
latest.script_key: horses show attention with their ears watch the ears before the body moves because each ear can turn toward a sound or animal the horse is tracking that small shift shows where attention is going before the next step the ears are a radar dish not decoration would you spot it

latest.title: Wild horses aren't farm animals they're athletes
latest.script_key: watch their hooves they land on one toe like elite sprinters their single toe is the speed secret not a hoof like we thought that s why they can outrun most predators did you learn this wrong too

latest.title: Sloth bears chew like vacuum cleaners
latest.script_key: watch this mouth vacuum that long snout isn t cute it s a 6 inch straw that sucks up termites like a dyson because their lips fold over those teeth they inhale without chomping anything but air did you think they were just clumsy grazers

latest.title: Turtles carry a magnetic map home
latest.script_key: turtles can navigate with earth s magnetic field watch the slow direction change because many turtles use magnetic information like a map that sense helps them cross huge distances and return toward important coasts later in life slow animal massive route which reptile journey next

latest.title: Snakes sample the air with a tongue flick
latest.script_key: snakes sample the air with a tongue flick watch the tongue flick because it collects scent particles and sends them to a special organ in the mouth that helps snakes track prey mates and danger without needing a loud chase the air is information would you notice the trail

latest.title: Cats measure tight spaces with their whiskers
latest.script_key: cats measure tight spaces with their whiskers watch the whiskers near the face because they sense tiny touches and air changes that helps cats judge gaps hunt in low light and protect their eyes before contact the face is doing math what pet sense should we test next

latest.title: Mangroves can slow waves before they hit land
latest.script_key: mangroves can slow waves before they hit land watch the roots in the water because mangroves create a rough barrier that breaks up wave energy they also shelter young fish and hold muddy coastlines together restoration can be infrastructure made of roots which repair story next

latest.title: Birds see ultraviolet patterns we miss
latest.script_key: birds reveal the ultraviolet vision first watch the ultraviolet vision first because many birds see ultraviolet detail hidden from us that extra color can help with mates food and navigation the same scene is richer to them than it is to us which bird fact next
